PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — the ORIGINAL host pull source, now the DUAL-PUSH + break-glass FALLBACK during the migration to self-hosted zot (ADR-096, #6122); retired from the pull path after the Phase-5 soak (App tokens can't pull GHCR, so it cannot deliver a zero-touch machine credential — ADR-088 superseded). Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime) AND a Logs warehouse (source 2457081, ClickHouse-SQL-queryable) that CI polls read-only. Apex + inngest + registry-disk heartbeats, plus (#6438/#6548) TWO new per-host web heartbeats — web_zot_consumer (the zot-consumer serviceability probe, #6438 §1) and web_nic_guard (the §3 private-NIC-guard liveness beat, #6438 §3), both for_each var.web_hosts — and git-data, which is now FED by the web-host git-data consumer probe (#6548): its git_data_prd beat was previously unfed (no probe cron was ever written) and absent from live Better Stack despite having no count gate, and is now armed via the ADR-117 measure-then-arm gate. Registry-liveness remains paused until #6537's feeder ships and a beat is measured. All three new/newly-fed web beats (web_zot_consumer, web_nic_guard, git_data_prd) are arm-gated by the automated measure-then-arm PATCH in apply-web-platform-infra.yml (ADR-117 amendment #6438). ALSO a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1). The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK + SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC self-reports from the deny-all registry host AND SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC from web-1 (the §3 web-host guard, detect+emit+alarm, NO reboot — ADR-123) and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is the whole `infra/sentry/**` tree and the plan is FULL-ROOT since #6589, so declaring a resource applies it and DELETING one really destroys it, gated pre-merge by `sentry-destroy-required` + `[ack-destroy]`. The filter was widened from a 3-file list because the retired `-target=` allowlist had doubled as the gate for declared-but-never-applied). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.

Keyless container-image signing/verification (cosign/Fulcio/Rekor). The release CI keyless-signs the pushed web app image digest (this IS a live sigstore call — github → sigstore below). The host then cosign-verifies the signature FULLY OFFLINE against a pinned trusted_root.json — it makes NO live sigstore call at verify time (ADR-087, #6005) — supply-chain authenticity for the shared host (#5933 Item 4).

platform.infra.tunnel.technology: cloudflared (ONE tunnel; web_tunnel_connector gate targets ONE connector but is construction-time only — TWO are live in prod: web-1 + web-2)

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.). DETERMINISM IS NOW STRUCTURAL, not one-connector: since #6594/PR-A all three ingress services are ORIGIN-RELATIVE (deploy. → http://10.0.1.10:9000, ssh. → ssh://10.0.1.10:22, registry. → tcp://10.0.1.30:5000), so whichever connector replica CF selects proxies to the correct origin (ADR-114 I2 discharged for all three). This — not 'localhost: means this host' — is what makes the management plane deterministic. NOT AN INVARIANT: the earlier 'exactly ONE connector, ADR-114 I1 enforced #6425' claim is FALSE in production. The server.tf web_tunnel_connector gate lives at cloud-init create-time user_data only, and hcloud_server.web carries ignore_changes=[user_data], so it never re-runs on a booted host; web-2 booted 2026-07-13 BEFORE the gate merged 2026-07-15, so TWO connectors are live (measured). I1 is a construction-time gate presented as a runtime precondition — it governs only future fresh hosts. CF binds ingress to a TUNNEL and picks a connector per edge colo; before the origin-relative pin a SECOND connector silently made every non-fan-out route (deploy-status, inngest-liveness, infra-config WRITEs, ssh.) answer from whichever replica the caller's colo selected — #6425 (16h of false inngest-down alarms), #6594 (a false-green infra-config apply), #6416. Origin-relative ingress makes that harmless: the coin flip no longer determines correctness. Standing guard: the */15 connector census in scheduled-inngest-health.yml (vantage-independent; a response-poll is colo-sticky and proves nothing). Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).

platform.infra.tunnel.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers. Per-user worktrees are host-local in the ADR-068 §1 sense (they do NOT live on the shared git-data host) but they are NOT on the root NVMe — they sit on a per-host ATTACHED BLOCK VOLUME, workspacesVolume below (#6588 corrected this description, which said 'host-local NVMe' and led readers to conclude there was no volume to encrypt). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921) — a path web-1 can never take: cx33 is unorderable in all 3 EU DCs and lifecycle{ignore_changes=[user_data]} means cloud-init never re-runs, so the bake reaches no live host (ADR-119 §(e))

platform.infra.workspacesVolume.technology: Hetzner Cloud block volume — ext4, mounted /mnt/data, bind-mounted into the app container as /workspaces

description.txt: Every user's checked-out repository. SOLE-COPY: refs/checkpoints/* is pushed by no refspec, session-sync.ts autocommits only knowledge-base/**, and signup-provisioned workspaces have no git remote at all — so ADR-068 §1's 'GitHub remains the durable rehydration source' does NOT hold here and there is no second copy anywhere. PLAINTEXT AT REST as of 2026-07-17 while the published privacy policy claims LUKS — the gap #6588/ADR-119 closes by cutting over to an additive LUKS volume (blue-green is impossible: cx33 unorderable ⇒ -replace would destroy the sole prod host and fail to recreate it). web-2's instance is knowingly left plaintext (never served, empty, slated for teardown #6538)

platform.infra.workspacesVolume.title: Workspace Git Data (/workspaces)

description.txt: Durable trigger layer for server-side agents — cron, oneshot, and HTTP-armed reminders. Extracted to its own single-host private-net node so exactly-one-scheduler is enforced by topology, unblocking active-active web (ADR-030, ADR-100, #6178)

platform.infra.inngestPostgres.technology: PostgreSQL — dedicated EU project, Supavisor session pooler :5432

description.txt: Inngest config + run history. Dedicated project isolates connection budget + fate from the main app DB (#5450). Public tables RLS-locked; reachable only as the postgres owner (ADR-030 I8)

platform.infra.inngestPostgres.title: Supabase PostgreSQL (Inngest)

description.txt: Inngest durable queue + run-state. AOF on the persistent volume survives a host re-provision — the armed-reminder durability fix (#5450)

Co-located stateless sticky router (in each web-host process, not a separate box) — resolves a session's owning host from the per-user worktree lease at the WS-upgrade handshake; local ⇒ serve, remote ⇒ proxy over one-way-TLS private net. Control ops are host-local (no cross-host forwarding). Lease in Postgres (ADR-068 user-sticky amendment)
Placement, health-reschedule, and rolling deploys across the Hetzner cluster (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

platform.infra.gitDataStore.technology: Bare git repos over private net

description.txt: Shared bare git repos (objects/refs) over the private net; per-user worktrees stay host-local on NVMe. The git-data host holds the per-ref monotonic max generation and writer-side-CAS-rejects stale lease writes (ADR-068)

platform.infra.gitDataStore.title: Shared git-data

1bs2yhc.title: deploy. → http://10.0.1.10:9000 (webhook) and ssh. → ssh://10.0.1.10:22 — ORIGIN-RELATIVE to web-1's private IP since #6594/PR-A (was localhost:, the ADR-114 I2 anti-pattern). 1fz0cwj.title: TWO web hosts run a cloudflared connector in prod (web-1 + web-2, measured). The server.tf web_tunnel_connector gate (#6425) was meant to leave only web-1, but it lives at cloud-init create-time user_data and hcloud_server.web has ignore_changes=[user_data], so it never re-ran on web-2 (booted 2026-07-13, before the gate merged 2026-07-15). So ssh:// and deploy. ingress are NOT deterministic by one-connector — they are deterministic because #6594/PR-A made their services origin-relative to web-1 (ADR-114 I2 discharged). CF still picks a connector per edge colo; the origin-relative pin makes that choice irrelevant to correctness. NOTE: the 'in-band hostname tripwire' this edge previously credited to the SSH provisioners does NOT exist — measured 2026-07-17, zero host-identity assertions in the 12 connection{} inlines (ADR-068:413's claim is false). The only retained absorption layer is ADR-068's private-net fan-out (deploy only).

mkk6w3.title: Stores every user's checked-out repository. Mounted at /mnt/data and bind-mounted into the app container as /workspaces (cloud-init.yml). The app container is the SOLE writer, which is what makes a container stop a sufficient freeze for the ADR-119 cutover — though `docker stop` defaults to a 10s grace then SIGKILL, so the cutover uses -t 120 plus interrupted-write asserts: a truncated file is otherwise rsynced and certified correct
mkk6w3.technology: ext4 on a Hetzner block volume (plaintext at rest — ADR-119 in progress)

1yscio3.title: Injects the web-host boot credential. The LUKS-at-rest passphrase (WORKSPACES_LUKS_KEY, ADR-119) is deliberately scoped to a DEDICATED prd_workspaces_luks config read by a read-only host token — NOT the shared soleur/prd config. Rationale is host-vs-CONTAINER, not host-vs-host (web-1 already carries full-prd, so there is no host blast radius left to buy): cloud-init runs `doppler secrets download --config prd` into the TMPENV that feeds `docker run --env-file`, so a key in shared prd would be readable via /proc/self/environ by the very agent code whose data it encrypts (CWE-522). The #6649 header-escrow R2 S3 creds (WORKSPACES_HEADER_R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID/_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) + bucket name + endpoint are delivered via the SAME prd_workspaces_luks scoped read — never on the cutover's sudo argv
1yscio3.technology: CLI (doppler run / scoped read-only service token)

13pvzz3.title: Uploads the LUKS header backup off-host to an R2 bucket (soleur-workspaces-luks-header) DISTINCT from the tfstate bucket (C4 escrow, ADR-119 #6649) — a BLOCKING pre-freeze gate: no off-host header ⇒ the cutover aborts before touching sole-copy data. LIMITATION: this single edge collapses BOTH R2 buckets (tfstate + header) into the one cloudflare node, so it does NOT visually encode the 'distinct blast radius' property — that lives only at runtime (workspaces-cutover.sh:185) + the workspaces-luks-header.test.sh reference-not-literal guard

mx0pd1.title: Ships disk-state observability — df% of /var/lib/zot + resize2fs before/after + zot health — as one SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK event, plus a SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC private-NIC convergence event (#6415), to the isolated Logs source 2457081; token in soleur-registry/prd, queried via betterstack-query.sh --grep SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK|SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC (#6244/#6415). ALSO pushes two distinct absence-based heartbeats, whose scopes must not be conflated: the 900s disk beat pings on df alone, so it alarms HOST death but stays green with zot dead; the 60s liveness beat (zot-liveness-heartbeat.timer, #6537) pings ONLY while zot answers on the host's own PRIVATE IP — never loopback, since zot binds 0.0.0.0 and loopback answers on a NIC-less host (#6400). The liveness beat's monitor stays PAUSED until #6537's post-merge reprovision measures a real beat and arms it; consumer-perspective reachability is unbuilt (#6438 §1)
mx0pd1.technology: curl → Better Stack Logs + heartbeats (HTTPS, eu-fsn-3)

oyzx3p.title: Polls the SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK + SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC Logs sources (ClickHouse SQL via betterstack-query.sh) for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm, the private-NIC convergence alarm (#6415), and follow-through soak probes. ALSO reads the heartbeats API read-only (GET /api/v2/heartbeats) from the scheduled-terraform-drift.yml heartbeat-live-reconcile job (#6549 item 2): it reconciles the LIVE paused/existence of each monitored heartbeat against the executable MANIFEST (heartbeat-manifest.ts) and flags fed-but-paused (the #6537 shape) or non-count-gated-absent-live (the #6548 git-data shape) — the live-state layer ADR-117's static guard cannot see. Distinct surface from the Logs poll above
oyzx3p.technology: HTTPS (ClickHouse SQL Logs + Uptime heartbeats API, eu-fsn-3)

1mmzv15.title: Host-side fatal + deploy telemetry, POSTed straight to the store API with the baked ${sentry_dsn} — the boot fatal-emit trap armed at the TOP of `runcmd:` in cloud-init.yml (before Doppler exists), ci-deploy.sh's deploy/sandbox-canary/cosign-verify/zot-fallback events, and container-restart-monitor.sh. Fail-open and SSH-free by construction: this is how a host that never finished booting still pages

1urh15b.title: Exceptions + debounced warns via the @sentry/nextjs SDK (sentry.client.config.ts / sentry.server.config.ts / instrumentation.ts, SENTRY_DSN) — the stack-keyed capture shape the issue alerts group on; AND the Inngest-fired crons' end-of-run check-ins (`postSentryHeartbeat` in _cron-shared.ts → /api/{id}/cron/{slug}/{key}/?status=ok|error, bounded retry #5728) that satisfy the 43 Inngest-substrate sentry_cron_monitors
1urh15b.technology: HTTPS (Sentry SDK envelope + cron check-in API)

1r6nbah.title: CI is both an emitter and the applier: the ./.github/actions/sentry-heartbeat composite posts ok|error check-ins from 6 workflows — 3 GHA-`schedule:`-fired (scheduled-inngest-health, -zot-restart-loop, -realtime-probe) and 3 `workflow_dispatch`-only that Inngest DISPATCHES per ADR-033 (scheduled-terraform-drift, -domain-model-drift, -supabase-advisor-scan). Those 3 are the third substrate: Inngest-scheduled but GHA-executed, so their check-in rides this edge, not webapp's. Of 50 cron monitors, 7 check in from here and 43 from webapp. apply-sentry-infra.yml also auto-applies the sentry root on push to main via the jianyuan/sentry provider (ADR-031 — no dashboard step; its `paths:` filter is the 3 rule files, not the whole root)
1r6nbah.technology: HTTPS (cron check-in API + Terraform provider, SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN)

1h8xvfw.title: Pages the operator — the LARGER of two independent exits (betterstack is the other; a Sentry outage is survivable BY DESIGN). Issue alerts route actions_v2 notify_email → target_type IssueOwners with fallthrough_type ActiveMembers — 21 of the 22 rules; `byok_cap_exceeded` deliberately sets fallthrough_type NoOne. NB IssueOwners has no ownership rule on this project, so the fallthrough is what actually reaches a human: a NoOne rule fires and pages nobody, by design there and a defect anywhere else. The cron monitors open an issue on a missed/failed check-in (failure_issue_threshold = 1) and the uptime monitors on downtime, both of which then fire that same email route

1sbx18a.title: Pages the operator INDEPENDENTLY of Sentry — the second source. The apex uptime monitor and the inngest + registry-disk heartbeats alert on downtime/missed beats (the git-data heartbeat does NOT — it is unfed and absent live, #6548; the registry LIVENESS heartbeat does not yet either — its feeder ships in #6537 and it is armed only once a real beat is measured post-reprovision); on the free tier this is email to the managed recipient, escalating via betteruptime_policy when var.betterstack_paid_tier is set. This is the exit that survives a Sentry-side outage, which is the entire reason the vendor is duplicated
1sbx18a.technology: Better Stack monitor/heartbeat → email (+ policy escalation on paid tier)

iz0bz4.title: Mints packages:read installation token every 20 min (ADR-088)

description.txt: A beta tester or prospect the operator is in sales/product conversations with. The origin of the third-party contact + conversation PII captured in the owner-private beta-CRM (beta_contacts / interview_notes). An involuntary data subject (Art. 14); no direct access to the store.

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime) AND a Logs warehouse (source 2457081, ClickHouse-SQL-queryable) that CI polls read-only. Apex + inngest + registry-disk heartbeats, plus (#6438/#6548) TWO new per-host web heartbeats — web_zot_consumer (the zot-consumer serviceability probe, #6438 §1) and web_nic_guard (the §3 private-NIC-guard liveness beat, #6438 §3), both for_each var.web_hosts — and git-data, which is now FED by the web-host git-data consumer probe (#6548): its git_data_prd beat was previously unfed (no probe cron was ever written) and absent from live Better Stack despite having no count gate, and is now armed via the ADR-117 measure-then-arm gate. Registry-liveness remains paused until #6537's feeder ships and a beat is measured. All three new/newly-fed web beats (web_zot_consumer, web_nic_guard, git_data_prd) are arm-gated by the automated measure-then-arm PATCH in apply-web-platform-infra.yml (ADR-117 amendment #6438). ALSO a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1). The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK + SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC self-reports from the deny-all registry host AND SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC from web-1 (the §3 web-host guard, detect+emit+alarm, NO reboot — ADR-123) and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is the whole `infra/sentry/**` tree and the plan is FULL-ROOT since #6589, so declaring a resource applies it and DELETING one really destroys it, gated pre-merge by `sentry-destroy-required` + `[ack-destroy]`. The filter was widened from a 3-file list because the retired `-target=` allowlist had doubled as the gate for declared-but-never-applied). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.
Vendors, regulators, and counsel emailing the operator address. Their statutory/operational mail is the email-triage inbox's inbound source.

An untrusted contributor opening a pull request. Their PR-head code is executed only by the fix-constraints Stage A producer (pull_request, contents:read, no write token) — never by the privileged Stage B consumer, which opens a data-only draft follow-up PR from the default branch (ADR-074).

The connected repo's own committed plugins/soleur/ dir, present in the user's workspace when they connect a Soleur-dogfooding repo. UNTRUSTED: its hooks/hooks.json command-hooks would run in the Node dispatch process (outside bwrap). INERT for the SDK — both real-SDK factories load plugins:[{path: getPluginPath()}] (the platform-deployed root), never this copy (ADR-093 trust boundary).

A workspace Owner using Soleur as their AI organization. Workspaces may have MULTIPLE Owners (ADR-038, ADR-073 team workspaces); Owner-shared surfaces (e.g. the operator email-triage inbox, ADR-066) are readable by every Owner, not just one founder.

Centralized secrets management with runtime injection
Community notifications and release announcements
Payment processing and subscription billing
Privacy-focused website analytics
Inbound email ingress (operator-inbox webhook, svix-verified) + transactional outbound (statutory triage notifications). ADR-066.

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — the ORIGINAL host pull source, now the DUAL-PUSH + break-glass FALLBACK during the migration to self-hosted zot (ADR-096, #6122); retired from the pull path after the Phase-5 soak (App tokens can't pull GHCR, so it cannot deliver a zero-touch machine credential — ADR-088 superseded). Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

Keyless container-image signing/verification (cosign/Fulcio/Rekor). The release CI keyless-signs the pushed web app image digest (this IS a live sigstore call — github → sigstore below). The host then cosign-verifies the signature FULLY OFFLINE against a pinned trusted_root.json — it makes NO live sigstore call at verify time (ADR-087, #6005) — supply-chain authenticity for the shared host (#5933 Item 4).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

nodes.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.). DETERMINISM IS NOW STRUCTURAL, not one-connector: since #6594/PR-A all three ingress services are ORIGIN-RELATIVE (deploy. → http://10.0.1.10:9000, ssh. → ssh://10.0.1.10:22, registry. → tcp://10.0.1.30:5000), so whichever connector replica CF selects proxies to the correct origin (ADR-114 I2 discharged for all three). This — not 'localhost: means this host' — is what makes the management plane deterministic. NOT AN INVARIANT: the earlier 'exactly ONE connector, ADR-114 I1 enforced #6425' claim is FALSE in production. The server.tf web_tunnel_connector gate lives at cloud-init create-time user_data only, and hcloud_server.web carries ignore_changes=[user_data], so it never re-runs on a booted host; web-2 booted 2026-07-13 BEFORE the gate merged 2026-07-15, so TWO connectors are live (measured). I1 is a construction-time gate presented as a runtime precondition — it governs only future fresh hosts. CF binds ingress to a TUNNEL and picks a connector per edge colo; before the origin-relative pin a SECOND connector silently made every non-fan-out route (deploy-status, inngest-liveness, infra-config WRITEs, ssh.) answer from whichever replica the caller's colo selected — #6425 (16h of false inngest-down alarms), #6594 (a false-green infra-config apply), #6416. Origin-relative ingress makes that harmless: the coin flip no longer determines correctness. Standing guard: the */15 connector census in scheduled-inngest-health.yml (vantage-independent; a response-poll is colo-sticky and proves nothing). Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).

nodes.technology: cloudflared (ONE tunnel; web_tunnel_connector gate targets ONE connector but is construction-time only — TWO are live in prod: web-1 + web-2)

Co-located stateless sticky router (in each web-host process, not a separate box) — resolves a session's owning host from the per-user worktree lease at the WS-upgrade handshake; local ⇒ serve, remote ⇒ proxy over one-way-TLS private net. Control ops are host-local (no cross-host forwarding). Lease in Postgres (ADR-068 user-sticky amendment)

Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers. Per-user worktrees are host-local in the ADR-068 §1 sense (they do NOT live on the shared git-data host) but they are NOT on the root NVMe — they sit on a per-host ATTACHED BLOCK VOLUME, workspacesVolume below (#6588 corrected this description, which said 'host-local NVMe' and led readers to conclude there was no volume to encrypt). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921) — a path web-1 can never take: cx33 is unorderable in all 3 EU DCs and lifecycle{ignore_changes=[user_data]} means cloud-init never re-runs, so the bake reaches no live host (ADR-119 §(e))

description.txt: Inngest durable queue + run-state. AOF on the persistent volume survives a host re-provision — the armed-reminder durability fix (#5450)
Source control, CI/CD, issue tracking, and releases
Centralized secrets management with runtime injection

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is the whole `infra/sentry/**` tree and the plan is FULL-ROOT since #6589, so declaring a resource applies it and DELETING one really destroys it, gated pre-merge by `sentry-destroy-required` + `[ack-destroy]`. The filter was widened from a 3-file list because the retired `-target=` allowlist had doubled as the gate for declared-but-never-applied). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime) AND a Logs warehouse (source 2457081, ClickHouse-SQL-queryable) that CI polls read-only. Apex + inngest + registry-disk heartbeats, plus (#6438/#6548) TWO new per-host web heartbeats — web_zot_consumer (the zot-consumer serviceability probe, #6438 §1) and web_nic_guard (the §3 private-NIC-guard liveness beat, #6438 §3), both for_each var.web_hosts — and git-data, which is now FED by the web-host git-data consumer probe (#6548): its git_data_prd beat was previously unfed (no probe cron was ever written) and absent from live Better Stack despite having no count gate, and is now armed via the ADR-117 measure-then-arm gate. Registry-liveness remains paused until #6537's feeder ships and a beat is measured. All three new/newly-fed web beats (web_zot_consumer, web_nic_guard, git_data_prd) are arm-gated by the automated measure-then-arm PATCH in apply-web-platform-infra.yml (ADR-117 amendment #6438). ALSO a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1). The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK + SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC self-reports from the deny-all registry host AND SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC from web-1 (the §3 web-host guard, detect+emit+alarm, NO reboot — ADR-123) and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).

nodes.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.). DETERMINISM IS NOW STRUCTURAL, not one-connector: since #6594/PR-A all three ingress services are ORIGIN-RELATIVE (deploy. → http://10.0.1.10:9000, ssh. → ssh://10.0.1.10:22, registry. → tcp://10.0.1.30:5000), so whichever connector replica CF selects proxies to the correct origin (ADR-114 I2 discharged for all three). This — not 'localhost: means this host' — is what makes the management plane deterministic. NOT AN INVARIANT: the earlier 'exactly ONE connector, ADR-114 I1 enforced #6425' claim is FALSE in production. The server.tf web_tunnel_connector gate lives at cloud-init create-time user_data only, and hcloud_server.web carries ignore_changes=[user_data], so it never re-runs on a booted host; web-2 booted 2026-07-13 BEFORE the gate merged 2026-07-15, so TWO connectors are live (measured). I1 is a construction-time gate presented as a runtime precondition — it governs only future fresh hosts. CF binds ingress to a TUNNEL and picks a connector per edge colo; before the origin-relative pin a SECOND connector silently made every non-fan-out route (deploy-status, inngest-liveness, infra-config WRITEs, ssh.) answer from whichever replica the caller's colo selected — #6425 (16h of false inngest-down alarms), #6594 (a false-green infra-config apply), #6416. Origin-relative ingress makes that harmless: the coin flip no longer determines correctness. Standing guard: the */15 connector census in scheduled-inngest-health.yml (vantage-independent; a response-poll is colo-sticky and proves nothing). Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).

nodes.technology: cloudflared (ONE tunnel; web_tunnel_connector gate targets ONE connector but is construction-time only — TWO are live in prod: web-1 + web-2)

Mutable read/act/archive state, workspace-grain, Owner-shared reads — SEPARATE from the email-triage WORM statutory ledger. Content-minimized (title + source_ref ids only). GET /api/inbox merges it with email-triage into one severity-ordered feed (statutory pinned first).

Owner-private beta-tester / prospect CRM (ADR-102, #6165). Third-party contact PII + dual-lens conversation notes + append-only stage-transition history. Owner-only RLS; writes RPC-only via auth.uid()-pinned SECURITY DEFINER functions; the cro/cpo agents read AND write via the crm_* MCP tools (agent-native parity). 24-month retention; Art. 17 via ON DELETE CASCADE + crm_erase_contact.

Self-hosted EU Redis — ADR-059 replay buffer + session/control state; network-reachable, per-workspace key namespacing, TLS + requirepass. DISTINCT node from the loopback Inngest Redis (ADR-068 Phase 4a)
Placement, health-reschedule, and rolling deploys across the Hetzner cluster (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

Co-located stateless sticky router (in each web-host process, not a separate box) — resolves a session's owning host from the per-user worktree lease at the WS-upgrade handshake; local ⇒ serve, remote ⇒ proxy over one-way-TLS private net. Control ops are host-local (no cross-host forwarding). Lease in Postgres (ADR-068 user-sticky amendment)

Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers. Per-user worktrees are host-local in the ADR-068 §1 sense (they do NOT live on the shared git-data host) but they are NOT on the root NVMe — they sit on a per-host ATTACHED BLOCK VOLUME, workspacesVolume below (#6588 corrected this description, which said 'host-local NVMe' and led readers to conclude there was no volume to encrypt). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921) — a path web-1 can never take: cx33 is unorderable in all 3 EU DCs and lifecycle{ignore_changes=[user_data]} means cloud-init never re-runs, so the bake reaches no live host (ADR-119 §(e))

Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is the whole `infra/sentry/**` tree and the plan is FULL-ROOT since #6589, so declaring a resource applies it and DELETING one really destroys it, gated pre-merge by `sentry-destroy-required` + `[ack-destroy]`. The filter was widened from a 3-file list because the retired `-target=` allowlist had doubled as the gate for declared-but-never-applied). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.

nodes.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.). DETERMINISM IS NOW STRUCTURAL, not one-connector: since #6594/PR-A all three ingress services are ORIGIN-RELATIVE (deploy. → http://10.0.1.10:9000, ssh. → ssh://10.0.1.10:22, registry. → tcp://10.0.1.30:5000), so whichever connector replica CF selects proxies to the correct origin (ADR-114 I2 discharged for all three). This — not 'localhost: means this host' — is what makes the management plane deterministic. NOT AN INVARIANT: the earlier 'exactly ONE connector, ADR-114 I1 enforced #6425' claim is FALSE in production. The server.tf web_tunnel_connector gate lives at cloud-init create-time user_data only, and hcloud_server.web carries ignore_changes=[user_data], so it never re-runs on a booted host; web-2 booted 2026-07-13 BEFORE the gate merged 2026-07-15, so TWO connectors are live (measured). I1 is a construction-time gate presented as a runtime precondition — it governs only future fresh hosts. CF binds ingress to a TUNNEL and picks a connector per edge colo; before the origin-relative pin a SECOND connector silently made every non-fan-out route (deploy-status, inngest-liveness, infra-config WRITEs, ssh.) answer from whichever replica the caller's colo selected — #6425 (16h of false inngest-down alarms), #6594 (a false-green infra-config apply), #6416. Origin-relative ingress makes that harmless: the coin flip no longer determines correctness. Standing guard: the */15 connector census in scheduled-inngest-health.yml (vantage-independent; a response-poll is colo-sticky and proves nothing). Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).

description.txt: Centralized secrets management with runtime injection

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime) AND a Logs warehouse (source 2457081, ClickHouse-SQL-queryable) that CI polls read-only. Apex + inngest + registry-disk heartbeats, plus (#6438/#6548) TWO new per-host web heartbeats — web_zot_consumer (the zot-consumer serviceability probe, #6438 §1) and web_nic_guard (the §3 private-NIC-guard liveness beat, #6438 §3), both for_each var.web_hosts — and git-data, which is now FED by the web-host git-data consumer probe (#6548): its git_data_prd beat was previously unfed (no probe cron was ever written) and absent from live Better Stack despite having no count gate, and is now armed via the ADR-117 measure-then-arm gate. Registry-liveness remains paused until #6537's feeder ships and a beat is measured. All three new/newly-fed web beats (web_zot_consumer, web_nic_guard, git_data_prd) are arm-gated by the automated measure-then-arm PATCH in apply-web-platform-infra.yml (ADR-117 amendment #6438). ALSO a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1). The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK + SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC self-reports from the deny-all registry host AND SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC from web-1 (the §3 web-host guard, detect+emit+alarm, NO reboot — ADR-123) and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).

nodes.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.). DETERMINISM IS NOW STRUCTURAL, not one-connector: since #6594/PR-A all three ingress services are ORIGIN-RELATIVE (deploy. → http://10.0.1.10:9000, ssh. → ssh://10.0.1.10:22, registry. → tcp://10.0.1.30:5000), so whichever connector replica CF selects proxies to the correct origin (ADR-114 I2 discharged for all three). This — not 'localhost: means this host' — is what makes the management plane deterministic. NOT AN INVARIANT: the earlier 'exactly ONE connector, ADR-114 I1 enforced #6425' claim is FALSE in production. The server.tf web_tunnel_connector gate lives at cloud-init create-time user_data only, and hcloud_server.web carries ignore_changes=[user_data], so it never re-runs on a booted host; web-2 booted 2026-07-13 BEFORE the gate merged 2026-07-15, so TWO connectors are live (measured). I1 is a construction-time gate presented as a runtime precondition — it governs only future fresh hosts. CF binds ingress to a TUNNEL and picks a connector per edge colo; before the origin-relative pin a SECOND connector silently made every non-fan-out route (deploy-status, inngest-liveness, infra-config WRITEs, ssh.) answer from whichever replica the caller's colo selected — #6425 (16h of false inngest-down alarms), #6594 (a false-green infra-config apply), #6416. Origin-relative ingress makes that harmless: the coin flip no longer determines correctness. Standing guard: the */15 connector census in scheduled-inngest-health.yml (vantage-independent; a response-poll is colo-sticky and proves nothing). Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).

nodes.technology: cloudflared (ONE tunnel; web_tunnel_connector gate targets ONE connector but is construction-time only — TWO are live in prod: web-1 + web-2)

description.txt: Placement, health-reschedule, and rolling deploys across the Hetzner cluster (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers. Per-user worktrees are host-local in the ADR-068 §1 sense (they do NOT live on the shared git-data host) but they are NOT on the root NVMe — they sit on a per-host ATTACHED BLOCK VOLUME, workspacesVolume below (#6588 corrected this description, which said 'host-local NVMe' and led readers to conclude there was no volume to encrypt). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921) — a path web-1 can never take: cx33 is unorderable in all 3 EU DCs and lifecycle{ignore_changes=[user_data]} means cloud-init never re-runs, so the bake reaches no live host (ADR-119 §(e))
DNS, CDN, R2 storage, and zero-trust tunnel

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — the ORIGINAL host pull source, now the DUAL-PUSH + break-glass FALLBACK during the migration to self-hosted zot (ADR-096, #6122); retired from the pull path after the Phase-5 soak (App tokens can't pull GHCR, so it cannot deliver a zero-touch machine credential — ADR-088 superseded). Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is the whole `infra/sentry/**` tree and the plan is FULL-ROOT since #6589, so declaring a resource applies it and DELETING one really destroys it, gated pre-merge by `sentry-destroy-required` + `[ack-destroy]`. The filter was widened from a 3-file list because the retired `-target=` allowlist had doubled as the gate for declared-but-never-applied). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.
Claude Code instances executing agent workflows

nodes.title: Workspace Git Data (/workspaces)

description.txt: Every user's checked-out repository. SOLE-COPY: refs/checkpoints/* is pushed by no refspec, session-sync.ts autocommits only knowledge-base/**, and signup-provisioned workspaces have no git remote at all — so ADR-068 §1's 'GitHub remains the durable rehydration source' does NOT hold here and there is no second copy anywhere. PLAINTEXT AT REST as of 2026-07-17 while the published privacy policy claims LUKS — the gap #6588/ADR-119 closes by cutting over to an additive LUKS volume (blue-green is impossible: cx33 unorderable ⇒ -replace would destroy the sole prod host and fail to recreate it). web-2's instance is knowingly left plaintext (never served, empty, slated for teardown #6538)

Durable trigger layer for server-side agents — cron, oneshot, and HTTP-armed reminders. Extracted to its own single-host private-net node so exactly-one-scheduler is enforced by topology, unblocking active-active web (ADR-030, ADR-100, #6178)
Source control, CI/CD, issue tracking, and releases

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime) AND a Logs warehouse (source 2457081, ClickHouse-SQL-queryable) that CI polls read-only. Apex + inngest + registry-disk heartbeats, plus (#6438/#6548) TWO new per-host web heartbeats — web_zot_consumer (the zot-consumer serviceability probe, #6438 §1) and web_nic_guard (the §3 private-NIC-guard liveness beat, #6438 §3), both for_each var.web_hosts — and git-data, which is now FED by the web-host git-data consumer probe (#6548): its git_data_prd beat was previously unfed (no probe cron was ever written) and absent from live Better Stack despite having no count gate, and is now armed via the ADR-117 measure-then-arm gate. Registry-liveness remains paused until #6537's feeder ships and a beat is measured. All three new/newly-fed web beats (web_zot_consumer, web_nic_guard, git_data_prd) are arm-gated by the automated measure-then-arm PATCH in apply-web-platform-infra.yml (ADR-117 amendment #6438). ALSO a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1). The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK + SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC self-reports from the deny-all registry host AND SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC from web-1 (the §3 web-host guard, detect+emit+alarm, NO reboot — ADR-123) and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).
Users, BYOK-encrypted API keys, conversation sessions
